defs: A cover; a shelter; a protection.

records.ety: [OE. hoveren, and hoven, prob. orig., to abide, linger, and fr. AS. hof house; cf. OFries. hovia to receive into one's house. See Hovel.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hovered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hovering.]

defs: To hang fluttering in the air, or on the wing; to remain in flight or floating about or over a place or object; to be suspended in the air above something.
quotes: Great flights of birds are hovering about the bridge, and settling on it.
A hovering mist came swimming o'er his sight.

defs: To hang about; to move to and fro near a place, threateningly, watchfully, or irresolutely.
quotes: Agricola having sent his navy to hover on the coast.
Hovering o'er the paper with her quill.
